title: Supply Chain Flow Visualization
prompt:
Create an interactive 3D supply chain flow visualization using Three.js that tracks avocados moving through a production pipeline. Load the 'Avocado.glb' model using GLTFLoader from a CDN (e.g., https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.160.0/).

SCENE SETUP:
- Camera: PerspectiveCamera(60, aspect, 0.1, 1000) positioned at (0, 12, 20) looking at origin.
- Lights: AmbientLight(0x404040, 0.6) and DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 1.0) at (10, 20, 10).
- Background color: 0x1a1a2e.
- OrbitControls enabled for camera.

SUPPLY CHAIN NODES (5 stations arranged in an arc):
1. FARM node at (-12, 0, 0) — green platform (BoxGeometry 3x0.5x3, color 0x2ecc71)
2. WAREHOUSE node at (-6, 0, -4) — orange platform (color 0xe67e22)
3. PROCESSING node at (0, 0, -6) — blue platform (color 0x3498db)
4. DISTRIBUTION node at (6, 0, -4) — purple platform (color 0x9b59b6)
5. RETAIL node at (12, 0, 0) — red platform (color 0xe74c3c)

Each node has a floating label (TextGeometry or a sprite) above it showing its name.
Connect consecutive nodes with curved tube paths (TubeGeometry along CatmullRomCurve3) colored 0x555555, radius 0.08.

AVOCADO ITEMS:
- Load 'Avocado.glb' and clone it to create supply items.
- Scale each avocado to (15, 15, 15) so it's visible.
- Avocados spawn at the FARM node and travel along the tube paths to each subsequent node.
- Maximum 10 avocados active at once.
- Each avocado takes 3 seconds to travel between nodes (use lerp along the curve path with a progress parameter 0→1).
- When an avocado reaches a node, it pauses for 1 second before continuing.
- When an avocado reaches RETAIL, it fades out (opacity 0) over 0.5s and is removed.

SIMULATION CONTROLS:
- A DOM button with id='btn-start' text 'Start' that begins the simulation (spawns avocados every 2 seconds).
- A DOM button with id='btn-pause' text 'Pause' that pauses all movement.
- A DOM button with id='btn-reset' text 'Reset' that clears all avocados and resets counters.
- A DOM slider with id='slider-speed' (range 0.5 to 3.0, step 0.1, default 1.0) that multiplies the speed of travel.
- A DOM element with id='speed-label' showing current speed multiplier.

INTERACTION:
- Clicking on any supply chain node (raycasting) selects it, highlighting it with emissive color 0xffff00, and shows its stats in a HUD panel.
- Press 'B' key to toggle a bottleneck at the PROCESSING node: when active, avocados take 3x longer at that node (3 seconds pause instead of 1). A red ring appears around the node.
- Press 'H' key to toggle a heatmap overlay: each node glows more intensely based on how many avocados have passed through it (use emissive intensity proportional to throughput count / 10, clamped to 1.0).

HUD PANEL (DOM overlay, top-right corner):
- id='hud-panel' showing: total spawned, total delivered (reached RETAIL), in-transit count, selected node name, selected node throughput.

STATE MANAGEMENT — Expose window.__3D_STATE__ with:
- simulationRunning: boolean
- paused: boolean
- speedMultiplier: number
- totalSpawned: number (avocados created)
- totalDelivered: number (avocados that reached RETAIL)
- inTransitCount: number (currently moving avocados)
- nodeThrough: {farm: n, warehouse: n, processing: n, distribution: n, retail: n}
- selectedNode: string|null (name of selected node)
- bottleneckActive: boolean
- heatmapActive: boolean
- avocadoPositions: array of {x,y,z} for each active avocado
- modelLoaded: boolean (true when Avocado.glb is loaded)

Update __3D_STATE__ every frame in the animation loop. Ensure all numerical values are never NaN.
physics_constraints: Avocados must follow the curved paths exactly (parametric t from 0 to 1). Speed multiplier scales travel time inversely. Bottleneck multiplies pause duration at PROCESSING by 3. Maximum 10 active avocados enforced. All position values must be finite (no NaN/Infinity). Throughput counts must be monotonically non-decreasing. totalSpawned >= totalDelivered + inTransitCount at all times.
